primary_accounting_issue: Possibilité d’appliquer une comptabilité de couverture en consolidation sur le risque de change d’un dividende intragroupe comptabilisé en créance

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the required precedence rule strictly and selected IFRS 9 as the governing lineage because hedge accounting models and hedged-item eligibility are directly governed by IFRS 9, while IAS 21 is relevant only as supporting authority on the consolidation consequences of foreign currency monetary items.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low — IFRS 9 directly governs hedge accounting models and eligibility, and IAS 21 complements rather than overlaps that model.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern hedge accounting and identify which items, including certain intragroup foreign currency exposures, can be designated as hedged items in consolidated financial statements.

supporting_authority.reason: This paragraph explains that exchange differences on intragroup monetary assets and liabilities are not fully eliminated on consolidation when group entities have different functional currencies.
This paragraph confirms the foreign currency translation treatment for monetary items, which frames the underlying FX exposure.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting models available for foreign currency exposures in consolidated financial statements
Recognised intragroup monetary item exposure versus forecast intragroup foreign currency exposure

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a top-level hedge accounting model and it is relevant for recognised foreign currency monetary exposures.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a top-level hedge accounting model and it is relevant where the FX exposure is linked to a highly probable forecast intragroup transaction.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct top-level hedge accounting model for foreign currency exposures in consolidated financial statements.
